spec_match.rationale: The demo correctly uses image(<color>) in both content (for generated avatars) and border-image (for edge accents).

interactive.rationale: Includes good interactive controls: oklch color switcher for avatars and a border-image layout override controller.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb aligns perfectly with the content and border-image concepts shown.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers multiple distinct properties (content, border-image) and various edge configurations (top, bottom, left, fat left).

references.rationale: Includes the mandatory ChromeStatus entry reference link.

design_system.rationale: Avatar initials now use var(--text-black), measuring 5.24:1 to 7.90:1 against every oklch avatar preset in the DevTools MCP verification pass.

summary: Repaired and verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148. Before the fix, ivory initials measured 2.59:1 to 3.90:1 against the five avatar color presets. After changing the initials to var(--text-black), the same presets measured 7.90:1, 6.12:1, 5.30:1, 5.24:1, and 6.22:1. The color buttons updated all five avatar variables, the border-image accent toggles still updated inline borderImage values, the page had no console errors or failed requests, and /v150/css-image-color-function/conformance/?verify=after-avatar-contrast-fix rendered 1 PASS / 7 FAIL because Chrome 148 does not support the v150 image(<color>) parser feature.
